meaning: adv.横跨着,斜穿过,逆,相反 prep.横过,反对,逆
examples: They put a table athwart the doorway.
His statement ran athwart what was previously said.
He said, sleepily, "Thou wilt sleep athwart the door, and guard it."
To be fair, a few politicians from both parties are standing athwart this self-inflicted madness yelling stop.
The region stands athwart three continents, Europe, Asia and Africa.
